LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICK POWER'S AMENDED MOTION TO STAY ACTION IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 13, 2013 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 29, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT POWER, PATRICK DANIEL
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-29-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: LASSART, JAMES A. SUBSTITUTED FOR LASSART, JAMES A. AS ATTORNEY FOR POWER, PATRICK DANIEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICK POWER'S AMENDED MOTION TO STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT DEFENDANT IS EXPOSED TO CRIMINAL PROSECUTION ARISING FROM THE SAME FACTS AS ALLEGED IN THE CIVIL CASE AS AN INTENTIONAL TORT. CONSIDERING THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE WHERE THE ENTIRE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE CIVIL CASE IS THE SAME AS THE PENDING CRIMINAL INVESTIGATION, THE BEST SOLUTION TO FAIRLY BALANCE THE INTERESTS OF THE PARTIES AND THE JUDICIAL SYSTEM IS TO STAY THE ACTION PENDING THE EXPIRATION OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OR THE RESOLUTION OF THE CRIMINAL PROSECUTION. FULLER V. SUPERIOR COURT, 87 CAL. APP. 4TH 299, 307-308 (2001).DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE INCIDENT IN QUESTION ALLEGEDLY OCCURRED ON JUNE 25, 2011. THE CIVIL LAWSUIT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL JUNE 12, 2013, NEARLY TWO YEARS LATER. THE CRIMINAL STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPEARS TO BE THREE YEARS (PENAL CODE 801), WHICH IS ABOUT 9 MONTHS FROM NOW. PLAINTIFF CONCURS THAT AN OPEN CRIMINAL INVESTIGATION IS "MORE THAN SUFFICIENT TO HAVE A VALID 5TH AMENDMENT PRIVILEGE TO ASSERT." (SEE PLAINTIFF'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION AT 5). A STAY WILL AVOID MULTIPLE PIECEMEAL MOTIONS WHICH ARE VIRTUALLY CERTAIN TO OCCUR GIVEN THE ALLEGATIONS IN THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
